items.question: itertools.chain vs concatenating lists with +?
items.answer: chain iterates several iterables lazily as one stream without building a combined list, so memory stays flat; list + list eagerly creates a new, fully materialized list.

items.question: Why might lru_cache return a wrong (stale) value?
items.answer: Because it memoizes by arguments. If the function is impure (depends on time, randomness, external state) or has side effects, cached results no longer reflect reality.

items.question: What breaks if you loop over an islice result twice?
items.answer: The second loop yields nothing — islice produces a one-pass iterator that's exhausted after the first consumption. Recreate it or store items in a list.
